items.title: One dead and dozens injured in train collision north of London
items.standfirst: One person was killed and dozens of others were injured after two passenger trains travelling towards London collided near Bedford on Friday evening, triggering an emergency response and suspending rail services to and from St Pancras station.

items.title: Trains collide in England, at least 1 killed, 89 injured
items.standfirst: Warning: Graphic video. Two passenger trains collided Friday in Bedford, England, outside of London. At least one person was killed and 89 others were injured, officials said.
items.content: Warning: Graphic video. Two passenger trains collided Friday in Bedford, England, outside of London. At least one person was killed and 89 others were injured, officials said.

items.title: 1 person killed, dozens injured after two trains collide north of London
items.standfirst: Two passenger trains collided north of London, with one person killed and dozens more hurt. Sky News’ Shamaan Freeman-Powell reports on the incident.
items.content: Two passenger trains collided north of London, with one person killed and dozens more hurt. Sky News’ Shamaan Freeman-Powell reports on the incident.

items.title: Train driver dies and 89 people injured after two trains collide near Bedford
items.standfirst: Eleven people suffer ‘very serious’ injuries, with emergency services still at the scene into the nightA train driver has died and 89 people have been injured after two trains collided in the Bedford area, with emergency services working into the night.Of those injured, 11 people suffered “very serious” injuries and 22 were seriously injured, East of England ambulance service said, while a further 56 people had minor injuries and were treated at the scene or taken to hospital. Continue reading...
items.content: Eleven people suffer ‘very serious’ injuries, with emergency services still at the scene into the nightA train driver has died and 89 people have been injured after two trains collided in the Bedford area, with emergency services working into the night.Of those injured, 11 people suffered “very serious” injuries and 22 were seriously injured, East of England ambulance service said, while a further 56 people had minor injuries and were treated at the scene or taken to hospital. Continue reading...

items.title: One dead and dozens injured after passenger trains collide north of London
items.standfirst: One person was killed and dozens were injured, including 11 seriously, after two London-bound passenger trains collided near Bedford on Friday. Emergency services mounted a major response, deploying air ambulances as investigators began examining the cause of one of Britain's most serious rail incidents in recent years.
items.content: One person was killed and dozens were injured, including 11 seriously, after two London-bound passenger trains collided near Bedford on Friday. Emergency services mounted a major response, deploying air ambulances as investigators began examining the cause of one of Britain's most serious rail incidents in recent years.

items.title: 'People flew from their seats': Passengers describe how collision unfolded
items.standfirst: The East of England Ambulance Service says nearly 90 people were injured, over 30 of them seriously.
items.content: The East of England Ambulance Service says nearly 90 people were injured, over 30 of them seriously.

clusters.title: Residents flee as Israeli strikes target southern Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, CBS News and others

items.title: Residents flee as Israeli strikes target southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah group announced a day earlier.
items.content: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah group announced a day earlier.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after deadly flareup disrupts opening of Iran talks
items.standfirst: JD Vance pulls out of US-Iran meeting in Switzerland on implementation of peace deal after clashes in southern LebanonMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...
items.content: JD Vance pulls out of US-Iran meeting in Switzerland on implementation of peace deal after clashes in southern LebanonMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill five despite ceasefire
items.standfirst: At least five people were killed in an Israeli air strike on southern Lebanon on Saturday, Lebanese state media said, after Israeli warplanes and drones reportedly carried out a series of attacks despite a ceasefire agreed with Hezbollah only a day earlier. The strikes came as US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi headed to Switzerland for talks. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: At least five people were killed in an Israeli air strike on southern Lebanon on Saturday, Lebanese state media said, after Israeli warplanes and drones reportedly carried out a series of attacks despite a ceasefire agreed with Hezbollah only a day earlier. The strikes came as US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi headed to Switzerland for talks. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Details on Israel and Hezbollah's ceasefire amid delayed U.S.-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon as officials say fighting between them has delayed U.S.-Iran peace talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Margaret Brennan report.
items.content: Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon as officials say fighting between them has delayed U.S.-Iran peace talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Margaret Brennan report.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
items.standfirst: The agreement followed concerns that continued clashes would undermine the deal to end the war between the US and Iran.
items.content: The agreement followed concerns that continued clashes would undermine the deal to end the war between the US and Iran.

items.title: Israel and Hizbollah agree ceasefire
items.standfirst: Oil price falls on hopes agreement will bolster US-Iran deal
items.content: Oil price falls on hopes agreement will bolster US-Iran deal

items.title: Expert warns of "bumpy road ahead" for Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire as U.S. seeks Iran deal
items.standfirst: CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean is reacting to the latest Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire news after tensions rose in Lebanon, likely leading to U.S.-Iran talks being postponed.
items.content: CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean is reacting to the latest Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire news after tensions rose in Lebanon, likely leading to U.S.-Iran talks being postponed.

items.title: US-Iran talks abruptly called off after Israel and Hezbollah trade deadly attacks
items.standfirst: JD Vance’s staff were at an airbase ready to fly to summit in Obbürgen before trip was suddenly cancelledMiddle East crisis – live updatesTalks due to take place on Friday between the US ⁠and ​Iran in Switzerland to implement a peace deal were cancelled as Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in south Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 18 people.The talks had been due to begin in the Swiss village of Obbürgen two days after the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU) that opened a 60-day window to negotiate a permanent understanding over Iran’s nuclear programme, while getting oil traffic moving through the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...
items.content: JD Vance’s staff were at an airbase ready to fly to summit in Obbürgen before trip was suddenly cancelledMiddle East crisis – live updatesTalks due to take place on Friday between the US ⁠and ​Iran in Switzerland to implement a peace deal were cancelled as Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in south Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 18 people.The talks had been due to begin in the Swiss village of Obbürgen two days after the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU) that opened a 60-day window to negotiate a permanent understanding over Iran’s nuclear programme, while getting oil traffic moving through the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire after intensified fighting threatens U.S.-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Israel and militant group Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday, after a deadly escalation between the two in Lebanon looked set to derail Washington-Tehran peace talks in Switzerland
items.content: Israel and militant group Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday, after a deadly escalation between the two in Lebanon looked set to derail Washington-Tehran peace talks in Switzerland

clusters.title: Top Italian diplomat cancels U.S. trip after prime minister slams Trump's claim about G7 photo
clusters.neutral_headline: Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo
bullet_summary: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back
Reported by NBC World, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Top Italian diplomat cancels U.S. trip after prime minister slams Trump's claim about G7 photo
items.standfirst: Italy's top diplomat canceled a trip to the U.S. after Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni slammed President Trump's claims that she "begged" him for a photo at the G7 summit. The dispute erupted after Trump told Italian broadcaster La7 that Meloni had pleaded with him for a photo at the meeting of leading industrialized nations in the French resort of Evian-les-Bains earlier this week.
items.content: Italy's top diplomat canceled a trip to the U.S. after Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni slammed President Trump's claims that she "begged" him for a photo at the G7 summit. The dispute erupted after Trump told Italian broadcaster La7 that Meloni had pleaded with him for a photo at the meeting of leading industrialized nations in the French resort of Evian-les-Bains earlier this week.

items.title: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
items.standfirst: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.
items.content: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.

items.title: Italy: Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo
items.standfirst: Donald Trump claimed that Giorgia Meloni had 'begged' for a photo with him during the recent G7 summit. He added that he wasn’t obliged to do it but that he felt sorry for her and agreed.
items.content: Donald Trump claimed that Giorgia Meloni had 'begged' for a photo with him during the recent G7 summit. He added that he wasn’t obliged to do it but that he felt sorry for her and agreed.

items.title: Trump ‘totally fabricated’ claim she begged him for a photo, Italy’s Meloni says
items.standfirst: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back.
items.content: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back.

items.title: Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo
items.standfirst: US president provokes outrage as PM says he ‘totally invented’ story in interview with Italian mediaItaly’s prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, has said Donald Trump “totally invented” a story about her after the US president claimed she begged him to take a photo with her during the G7 summit.The two former allies had appeared to be getting their relationship back on track by having several one-to-ones on the sidelines at the gathering in Évian after falling out in April over the US-Israeli war in Iran. Continue reading...
items.content: US president provokes outrage as PM says he ‘totally invented’ story in interview with Italian mediaItaly’s prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, has said Donald Trump “totally invented” a story about her after the US president claimed she begged him to take a photo with her during the G7 summit.The two former allies had appeared to be getting their relationship back on track by having several one-to-ones on the sidelines at the gathering in Évian after falling out in April over the US-Israeli war in Iran. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Completely made up': Italy's PM on Trump's comments she begged him for photo
items.standfirst: Italian Prime ​Minister Giorgia Meloni has accused Donald Trump of making up a story about her, after ‌the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a picture with her at the G7 summit in France.
items.content: Italian Prime ​Minister Giorgia Meloni has accused Donald Trump of making up a story about her, after ‌the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a picture with her at the G7 summit in France.

clusters.title: Western Europe braces for more extreme heat as temperatures continue to climb
clusters.neutral_headline: Macron calls for vigilance as western Europe faces second heatwave of year
bullet_summary: “We are going through difficult days,” Emmanuel Macron said

items.title: Western Europe braces for more extreme heat as temperatures continue to climb
items.standfirst: Western Europe sweltered under a punishing heatwave on Friday, with temperatures expected to climb further in the coming days and potentially shatter more records, prompting authorities across the continent to issue warnings and brace for prolonged extreme weather. In France, hundreds of schools were forced to tweak their timetables to cope.
items.content: Western Europe sweltered under a punishing heatwave on Friday, with temperatures expected to climb further in the coming days and potentially shatter more records, prompting authorities across the continent to issue warnings and brace for prolonged extreme weather. In France, hundreds of schools were forced to tweak their timetables to cope.

items.title: Macron calls for vigilance as western Europe faces second heatwave of year
items.standfirst: More than half of France’s population under severe weather warning with temperatures expected to exceed 40CMore than half of France’s population is under a severe weather warning as large swathes of western Europe endure the second extreme heat event of the year, with temperatures expected to exceed 40C (104F).The French president called for “extreme vigilance”, urging people to “take care of our oldest and most vulnerable people” and follow government advice. “We are going through difficult days,” Emmanuel Macron said. Continue reading...
items.content: More than half of France’s population under severe weather warning with temperatures expected to exceed 40CMore than half of France’s population is under a severe weather warning as large swathes of western Europe endure the second extreme heat event of the year, with temperatures expected to exceed 40C (104F).The French president called for “extreme vigilance”, urging people to “take care of our oldest and most vulnerable people” and follow government advice. “We are going through difficult days,” Emmanuel Macron said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Morocco beat Scotland 1–0 as Saibari scores fastest World Cup 2026 goal
clusters.neutral_headline: Morocco beat Scotland 1–0 as Saibari scores fastest World Cup 2026 goal

items.title: Morocco beat Scotland 1–0 as Saibari scores fastest World Cup 2026 goal
items.standfirst: Morocco hold onto slender lead despite Scotland's late charge for an equaliser in Group C match at Boston Stadium.
items.content: Morocco hold onto slender lead despite Scotland's late charge for an equaliser in Group C match at Boston Stadium.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Saibari's early goal hands Morocco narrow win over Scotland
items.standfirst: Ismael Saibari scored the fastest goal of the World Cup so far after just 71 seconds as Morocco secured a narrow 1-0 win over Scotland on Friday to close in on a place in the knockout round. The Scots pushed hard for an equaliser in the ⁠secondhalf but never had a shot on target during the match.
items.content: Ismael Saibari scored the fastest goal of the World Cup so far after just 71 seconds as Morocco secured a narrow 1-0 win over Scotland on Friday to close in on a place in the knockout round. The Scots pushed hard for an equaliser in the ⁠secondhalf but never had a shot on target during the match.

items.content: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

items.title: James Burrows, co-creator of "Cheers" and prolific director, dies at 85
items.standfirst: James Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of television, including every episode of the original "Will & Grace."
items.content: James Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of television, including every episode of the original "Will & Grace."

items.title: "Cheers" co-creator James Burrows dies at 85
items.standfirst: Director James Burrows co-created the landmark comedy "Cheers" and directed more than 50 pilot episodes. He died at 85 on Friday.
items.content: Director James Burrows co-created the landmark comedy "Cheers" and directed more than 50 pilot episodes. He died at 85 on Friday.

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, gifted from Qatar
items.standfirst: President Trump unveiled what's meant to become the new Air Force One, a gift from the government of Qatar. Plus, new fighting erupted between Israel and Lebanon before they signed a new ceasefire. Nicole Killion has all this and more from the White House.
items.content: President Trump unveiled what's meant to become the new Air Force One, a gift from the government of Qatar. Plus, new fighting erupted between Israel and Lebanon before they signed a new ceasefire. Nicole Killion has all this and more from the White House.

items.title: Air Force One, gifted to Trump from Qatar, arrives at Joint Base Andrews
items.standfirst: The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.
items.content: The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government. (Image credit: Alex Wong)

items.title: Trump unveils Qatari-gifted jet serving as new Air Force One

items.content: President Trump on Friday unveiled the new Air Force One, a $400 million Boeing 747-8 jet, gifted by Qatar.
